name: Historgram
description: A simple histogram visualization component for displaying data distribution with range highlighting.
usage_guidelines: Use for visualizing data distribution across bins.
Highlight specific range with active state.
Bars scale relative to maximum value.
Useful for range selectors and data analysis.

data.description: Array of data points with values and labels.

styling_info.visual_description: Vertical bars with heights proportional to values. Bars within active range use primary color, others use gray. Smooth color transitions.

Dont: Don't use for time series (use line chart)

Basic.title: Basic
Basic.code:
import { useState } from 'react' import Histogram from '@/components/shared/Historgram' import Slider from '@/components/ui/Slider' const sampleData = [ { value: 5, label: '0-10' }, { value: 12, label: '10-20' }, { value: 25, label: '20-30' }, { value: 38, label: '30-40' }, { value: 45, label: '40-50' }, { value: 32, label: '50-60' }, { value: 28, label: '60-70' }, { value: 15, label: '70-80' }, { value: 8, label: '80-90' }, { value: 3, label: '90-100' }, ] const Basic = () => { const [range, setRange] = useState<[number, number]>([20, 70]) return (
Range: {range[0]} - {range[1]}
Min
setRange([value as number, range[1]]) } />
Max
setRange([range[0], value as number]) } />
) } export default Basic
